label: Null-return from liveOwnerForService is sole FAIL arbiter; genuine crash FAILs only when isPidAlive returns false for all lease pids
isPidAlive: ESRCH->false (dead), EPERM->true (alive), no-error->true (alive) — correctly implemented
PID-recycling attack: if daemon crashes without cleaning lease AND OS recycles ownerPid to a live unrelated process, isPidAlive returns true and verdict becomes SKIP instead of FAIL — residual risk
DR-016 childPid not checked by harness: launcher crash with live child -> harness returns null -> FAIL (correct for harness purpose, but not a false-green)
EPERM from process.kill(pid,0) does NOT independently mask genuine crash beyond the PID-recycling risk in F-003; EPERM always means a real live process exists

direction: EPERM false-green independent of PID recycling
reason: EPERM always means process is alive; EPERM cannot cause false-green except via the PID-recycling path (F-003) which requires a dead daemon's PID to be live-reassigned

direction: TOCTOU between connect failure and lease read
reason: liveOwnerForService is called synchronously in catch block with no await between client.close() and lease read; no meaningful race window
childPid not checked means harness FAILs when launcher is dead even if child is alive; not a false-green, but the harness purpose is launcher's ability to spawn a dedicated child
